sections.paragraph: τεταρταῖος, α, ον [cp. τέταρτος] ‘involved w. someth. relating to the numerical value of four’: the adj. is commonly used with ἡμέρα and understood as ‘next after three days’ in sense of fourth, but in J 11:39 Lazarus is viewed as agent τεταρταῖός ἐστιν (lit.) he’s a four-dayer, in context = he’s been doing his fourth day dead or he’s been in the tomb four days.
